en: And God spoke all these words, saying,
zh: 神吩咐以下這一切話，說：

en: "I am the LORD your God, who brought you out of the land of Egypt, out of the house of slavery.
zh: “我是耶和華你的 神，曾經把你從埃及地，從為奴之家領出來。

en: "You shall have no other gods before me.
zh: “除我以外，你不可有別的神。

en: "You shall not make for yourself a carved image, or any likeness of anything that is in heaven above, or that is in the earth beneath, or that is in the water under the earth.
zh: “不可為自己做偶像，也不可做天上、地下和地底下水中各物的形象。

en: You shall not bow down to them or serve them, for I the LORD your God am a jealous God, visiting the iniquity of the fathers on the children to the third and the fourth generation of those who hate me,
zh: 不可跪拜它們，也不可事奉它們，因為我耶和華你們的 神是忌邪的 神；恨惡我的，我必追討他們的罪，從父親到兒子，直到三四代。

en: but showing steadfast love to thousands of those who love me and keep my commandments.
zh: 愛我和遵守我誡命的，我必向他們施慈愛，直到千代。

en: "You shall not take the name of the LORD your God in vain, for the LORD will not hold him guiltless who takes his name in vain.
zh: “不可妄稱耶和華你 神的名，因為妄稱耶和華的名的，耶和華必不以他為無罪。

en: "Remember the Sabbath day, to keep it holy.
zh: “要記念安息日，守為聖日。

en: Six days you shall labor, and do all your work,
zh: 六日要勞碌，作你一切工作。

en: but the seventh day is a Sabbath to the LORD your God. On it you shall not do any work, you, or your son, or your daughter, your male servant, or your female servant, or your livestock, or the sojourner who is within your gates.
zh: 但第七日是耶和華你的 神的安息日；這一日，你和你的兒女，你的僕婢和牲畜，以及住在你城裡的寄居者，不可作任何的工。

en: For in six days the LORD made heaven and earth, the sea, and all that is in them, and rested the seventh day. Therefore the LORD blessed the Sabbath day and made it holy.
zh: 因為耶和華在六日之內造天、地、海和其中的萬物，第七日就歇息了；所以耶和華賜福安息日，定為聖日。

en: "Honor your father and your mother, that your days may be long in the land that the LORD your God is giving you.
zh: “要孝敬父母，使你在耶和華你的 神賜給你的地上得享長壽。

en: "You shall not murder.
zh: “不可殺人。

en: "You shall not commit adultery.
zh: “不可姦淫。

en: "You shall not steal.
zh: “不可偷盜。

en: "You shall not bear false witness against your neighbor.
zh: “不可作假證供陷害你的鄰舍。

en: "You shall not covet your neighbor's house; you shall not covet your neighbor's wife, or his male servant, or his female servant, or his ox, or his donkey, or anything that is your neighbor's."
zh: “不可貪愛你鄰舍的房屋；不可貪愛你鄰舍的妻子、僕婢、牛驢和他的任何東西。”

en: Now when all the people saw the thunder and the flashes of lightning and the sound of the trumpet and the mountain smoking, the people were afraid and trembled, and they stood far off
zh: 全體人民都看見打雷、閃電、角聲和冒著煙的山；人民看見了，就顫抖，遠遠地站著。

en: and said to Moses, "You speak to us, and we will listen; but do not let God speak to us, lest we die."
zh: 他們對摩西說：“求你和我們說話吧，我們必定聽你的話，不可讓 神和我們說話，恐怕我們死亡。”

en: Moses said to the people, "Do not fear, for God has come to test you, that the fear of him may be before you, that you may not sin."
zh: 摩西回答人民：“不要懼怕，因為 神降臨是要試驗你們，叫你們常常敬畏他，不至犯罪。”

en: The people stood far off, while Moses drew near to the thick darkness where God was.
zh: 於是，人民遠遠地站著，摩西卻走近 神所在的幽暗之中。

en: And the LORD said to Moses, "Thus you shall say to the people of Israel: 'You have seen for yourselves that I have talked with you from heaven.
zh: 耶和華對摩西說：“你要對以色列人這樣說：‘你們親自看見我從天上和你們說話了。

en: You shall not make gods of silver to be with me, nor shall you make for yourselves gods of gold.
zh: 你們不可做銀的神像和金的神像和我相比，你們決不可為自己做這些神像。

en: An altar of earth you shall make for me and sacrifice on it your burnt offerings and your peace offerings, your sheep and your oxen. In every place where I cause my name to be remembered I will come to you and bless you.
zh: 你要為我築一座土壇，把你的燔祭、平安祭和牛羊獻在壇上；在我使人記念我名的地方，我都必到你那裡去賜福你。

en: If you make me an altar of stone, you shall not build it of hewn stones, for if you wield your tool on it you profane it.
zh: 你若是為我築一座石壇，不可用鑿過的石頭來建築，因為你在石頭上一動用工具，就把壇污穢了。

en: And you shall not go up by steps to my altar, that your nakedness be not exposed on it.'
zh: 你上我的壇，不可用梯級，免得在壇上露出你的下體來。’”
